lede: A look at July 21 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Emperor Wen of Sui and James Cooke Brown, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Joe Biden
events.desc: U.S. President Joe Biden announces he will no longer seek a second term and withdraws from the 2024 election, endorsing Vice President Kamala Harris as she launches her own presidential campaign.

events.title: Barack Obama
events.desc: U.S. President Barack Obama signs the Dodd–Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: Bloody Friday: The Provisional IRA detonate 22 bombs in central Belfast, Northern Ireland, United Kingdom in the space of 80 minutes, killing nine and injuring 130.

events.title: Sirimavo Bandaranaike
events.desc: Sirimavo Bandaranaike is sworn in as prime minister of Ceylon, becoming the world's first democratically elected female head of government.

events.title: 1952 Kern County earthquake
events.desc: The 7.3 Mw Kern County earthquake strikes Southern California with a maximum Mercalli intensity of XI (Extreme), killing 12 and injuring hundreds.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Battle of Guam: American troops land on Guam, starting a battle that will end on August 10.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: First Battle of Bull Run: At Manassas Junction, Virginia, the first major battle of the war begins and ends in a victory for the Confederate army.

events.title: Qing dynasty
events.desc: Qing dynasty regent Dorgon issues an edict ordering all Han Chinese men to shave their forehead and braid the rest of their hair into a queue identical to those of the Manchus.

events.title: AD 365
events.desc: The 365 Crete earthquake affected the Greek island of Crete with a maximum Mercalli intensity of XI (Extreme), causing a destructive tsunami that affects the coasts of Libya and Egypt, especially Alexandria. Many thousands are killed.

events.title: Pope Pontian
events.desc: Pope Pontian succeeds Urban I as the eighteenth pope. After being exiled to Sardinia, he became the first pope to resign his office.

births.name: Ernest Hemingway
births.line: American novelist, short story writer, and journalist, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Anna de' Medici, Archduchess of Austria
John Atta Mills
births.line: Ghanaian lawyer and politician, 3rd President of Ghana

deaths.text: Philipp Frank, Austrian-American physicist, mathematician, and philosopher, Vienna Circle member
Claus von Stauffenberg, German soldier who attempted to assassinate Adolf Hitler
François Sébastien Charles Joseph de Croix, Count of Clerfayt, Austrian field marshal

observances.text: Christian feast day: Albert John Luthuli (Episcopal Church)
Christian feast day: Carlos of Brazil (Brazilian Catholic Apostolic Church)
